userStories.title: Implement devtimer start command
userStories.description: As a developer, I want to run 'devtimer start' to begin logging a coding session for the current repo.
acceptanceCriteria: Running 'devtimer start' inside a git repo inserts a row in sessions with started_at = now() and ended_at = null
Project is auto-registered if not seen before (uses get_or_create_project)
Prints: 'Started session for <project-name> at <HH:MM>'
If a session is already active for this project, prints an error: 'Session already active for <project-name>. Run devtimer stop first.' and exits non-zero
Running outside a git repo prints: 'Not inside a git repository' and exits non-zero
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: Implement devtimer stop command
userStories.description: As a developer, I want to run 'devtimer stop' to close the active session and record how long I worked.
acceptanceCriteria: Running 'devtimer stop' sets ended_at = now() on the most recent open session for the current project
Prints: 'Stopped. Session duration: <Xh Ym>'
If no active session exists, prints: 'No active session for <project-name>' and exits non-zero
Accepts optional --note flag: 'devtimer stop --note "Fixed login bug"' saves the note text to the sessions.notes column
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: Implement devtimer status command
userStories.description: As a developer, I want to run 'devtimer status' to see whether a session is currently active.
acceptanceCriteria: If a session is active: prints 'Active session: <project-name> — running for <Xh Ym> (started <HH:MM>)'
If no session is active: prints 'No active session'
Works correctly even when run from a directory that is not a git repo
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: Implement devtimer log command
userStories.description: As a developer, I want to run 'devtimer log' to review my recent sessions in a readable table.
acceptanceCriteria: Prints the 10 most recent completed sessions as a table with columns: Date, Project, Duration, Notes
Duration formats as '1h 23m' or '45m' (no hours if under 60 min)
In-progress sessions are shown with duration '(in progress)' and no ended_at
Accepts optional --project flag to filter by project name: 'devtimer log --project myapp'
Accepts optional --limit flag to change result count: 'devtimer log --limit 25'
Typecheck passes
